docs.title: Welcome to Ikirati
docs.text: Ikirati is a Cultural Heritage Platform designed to preserve and showcase the rich heritage of the Ikirati community. This platform allows for the collection, meaningful organization, and accessible presentation of cultural stories, artifacts, and traditions.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The project consists of two main components:

  • Backend: A Node.js/Express server that manages data persistence, authentication, and API endpoints.
  • Frontend: A React application (built with Vite) that provides an interactive user interface for exploring and managing cultural content.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js (v18 or higher)
  • npm or yarn

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: bash git clone <repository-url> cd ikirati

  2. Install dependencies for all packages: bash npm run install:all

  3. Start the development servers: bash npm run dev

This will launch both the backend and frontend in development mode. - Frontend: http://localhost:5173 - Backend: http://localhost:3000

docs.title: Backend Documentation
docs.text:
The backend service is built with Node.js, Express, and MySQL (mysql2). It provides JWT-based authentication and a stories API, and in production serves the built frontend as static files.

Note: Site content (stories, recipes, songs, elders, vocabulary, events, crafts, gallery) is managed in Sanity CMS and fetched directly by the frontend. The Express API is a standalone community-stories backend and is not currently consumed by the frontend.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Navigate to the backend directory:

cd backend

Install dependencies:

npm install

For development:

npm run dev

For production (build first):

npm run build
npm start

Lint and test:

npm run lint
npm test

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the backend directory based on .env.example.

Variable Description PORT The port the server runs on (default: 4000) NODE_ENV development or production JWT_SECRET Secret key for JWT signing. Required in production — the server exits if unset or set to a known placeholder. FRONTEND_URL Allowed CORS origin (default: http://localhost:5173) TRUST_PROXY Set to 1 when behind a reverse proxy/load balancer so rate limiting keys on real client IPs DB_HOST / DB_PORT MySQL host and port (default: localhost:3306) DB_USER / DB_PASSWORD MySQL credentials. Required in production. DB_NAME Database name (default: ikirati) RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS / RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS General rate limiter AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS / AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS Stricter limiter for /api/auth

docs.title: API Structure
docs.text: The API is organized into routes located in src/routes.

docs.title: /api/auth
docs.text: Method Path Auth Description POST /register public Register (email + strong password). Always forces role: user. POST /login public Returns a JWT (1 day expiry) and the user object. GET /profile Bearer token Returns the decoded token user.

docs.title: /api/stories
docs.text:
Method Path Auth Description GET / public List all stories GET /:id public Get one story GET /user/me Bearer token Current user's stories POST / Bearer token Create a story PUT /:id owner or admin Update a story DELETE /:id owner or admin Delete a story

Unmatched /api/* paths return a JSON 404; unexpected errors return a generic JSON 500 (details are logged server-side only).

docs.title: Database
docs.text: Tables (users, stories) are created automatically on startup by initDatabase() in src/db.ts. There is no migration tool — schema changes are made by editing that function.

docs.title: Frontend Documentation
docs.text: The frontend is a single-page application built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. It serves as the user-facing interface for the Ikirati platform.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Navigate to the frontend directory:

cd frontend

Install dependencies:

npm install

Start the development server:

npm run dev

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Framework: React
  • Build Tool: Vite
  • Language: TypeScript
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS
  • Routing: React Router

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
• src/components: Reusable UI components
  • src/pages: Route components (HomePage, CraftsPage, EldersPage, etc.)
  • src/data: Static data or data types
  • src/assets: Static assets like images and fonts

docs.title: Building for Production
docs.text:
To create a production build:

npm run build

The output will be in the dist directory.

docs.title: Sanity Studio Documentation
docs.text: The studio/ directory contains the Sanity Studio used to manage all site content. The frontend fetches this content directly from Sanity's CDN via GROQ queries.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
cd studio
npm install
npm run dev   # Studio at http://localhost:3333

Deploy the hosted studio:

npm run deploy

docs.title: Content Types
docs.text:
Schema Used by page Key fields story Stories (source: legend), Archive (source: cultural-story) title, text, author, image, link, source recipe Archive → Recipes name, difficulty, time, content, ingredients, prep, image song Archive → Songs name, type, audio (file), duration, content, link (YouTube) elder Elders name, age, specialty, quote, image, audio vocabularyEntry Language word, language (Limbu/Rai/Yakkha/Sunuwar), translation, pronunciation, example, audio event Events title, date, location, eventType, description, link craft Crafts title, description, image, difficulty, timeToLearn galleryImage Gallery title, description, image, category

Pages show a friendly "coming soon" empty state until documents of the matching type are published.

docs.title: Frontend connection
docs.text: The frontend reads VITE_SANITY_PROJECT_ID and VITE_SANITY_DATASET from frontend/.env (see frontend/.env.example). Never put a write token (SANITY_API_TOKEN) in a VITE_-prefixed variable — anything with that prefix ships in the public bundle.
